name: linkup_client_research
description: Run client_id-scoped research via Linkup for a single client in the fleet, seeded from their intake (name, LinkedIn/site/resume URLs): career signals, achievement signals, and 3-5 real voice reference excerpts of the client's own writing (LinkedIn posts, blog prose, resume — never AI-generated or paraphrased). Feeds the researcher specialist's client_research artifact, which every downstream artifact (positioning_brief, ClientBrandMemory, site_copy, post_draft) traces claims and voice back to. Every returned claim is claim_status-tagged (supported/needs_source/remove) per ANTI-FABRICATION policy.md; fewer than 3 usable voice_reference_excerpts is a known automatic upstream fail at voice_qa (repair_target: researcher), so this tool always returns whatever excerpts it genuinely found even below that threshold rather than padding or paraphrasing to reach 3. Fails clearly (fail-loud, non-retryable message) when LINKUP_API_KEY is unavailable rather than silently degrading or returning empty signal arrays that could be mistaken for a genuine 'no findings' result.

client_id.description: Fleet client identifier this research is scoped to; threaded through to output and downstream fleet events. Never joined against another client_id's state (FLEET ISOLATION).

fleet_id.description: ID of the parent fleet run this research call belongs to, recorded on the resulting fleet event.

name.description: Client's full name, used to seed the query and disambiguate search results.

linkedin_url.description: Client's LinkedIn profile URL from intake, if known. Primary source for voice_reference_excerpts (post text) and career_signals.

site_url.description: Client's personal or company site URL from intake, if known, used to widen source coverage for career/achievement signals and additional voice excerpts (blog prose).

resume_url.description: Client's resume/CV URL from intake, if known. Primary source for career_signals and achievement_signals; secondary source for voice excerpts.

focus_areas.description: Which research facets to run this call; defaults to all three when omitted. 'voice' targets voice_reference_excerpts collection specifically, useful on a deep retry after a voice_qa upstream fail.

depth.description: Linkup search depth. 'standard' for routine intake, 'deep' for retry/escalation passes (e.g. after an upstream voice_qa fail for insufficient voice_reference_excerpts).

include_domains.description: Optional domain allowlist to bias search sources (e.g. the client's own site_url, linkedin.com, the client's resume host).

voice_reference_excerpts.description: Real, verbatim excerpts of the client's own writing. May legitimately contain fewer than 3 entries for a thin-source client; this tool never pads or paraphrases to reach the voice_qa minimum.

text.description: Verbatim excerpt text, never AI-generated or paraphrased.

excerpt_id.description: Stable id (e.g. vre_1) other artifacts reference by.

usable_claim_count.description: Researcher-facing rollup: count across career_signals + achievement_signals combined with claim_status: supported.

needs_source_count.description: Rollup count across career_signals + achievement_signals combined with claim_status: needs_source.

removed_count.description: Rollup count across career_signals + achievement_signals combined with claim_status: remove.

usable_voice_excerpt_count.description: Count of voice_reference_excerpts returned; must be >= 3 for voice_qa to accept downstream candidates without an automatic upstream fail.
